headlines.title: Raised Estimates Before Earnings: Is Old Dominion (ODFL) Redefining Pricing Power in a Tough Freight Market?
headlines.summary: Old Dominion Freight Line recently attracted attention ahead of its July 29, 2026 second-quarter earnings release, as analysts raised their earnings and revenue estimates and pointed to the company’s cost-based pricing and disciplined yield management amid ongoing geopolitical and supply-chain challenges.

headlines.title: What Makes Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) a Strong Momentum Stock: Buy Now?
headlines.summary: Does Old Dominion Freight Line (ODFL) have what it takes to be a top stock pick for momentum investors? Let's find out.

headlines.title: Old Dominion Freight Line Announces $0.29 Per Share Quarterly Cash Dividend
headlines.summary: THOMASVILLE, N.C., July 23, 2026--Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. (Nasdaq: ODFL) today announced that its Board of Directors has declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.29 per share of common stock, payable on September 16, 2026, to shareholders of record at the close of business on September 2, 2026. This dividend payment represents a 3.6% increase to the quarterly cash dividend paid in September 2025.

headlines.title: Is FDXF Stock a Buy With Growth Targets Amid High Debt and Macro Risks
headlines.summary: FDXF pairs scale, pricing tools and growth targets with high debt, soft demand and spin-off execution risks, making it a hold currently.

description: Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. operates as a less-than-truckload motor carrier in the United States and North America. The company offers regional, inter-regional, and national less-than-truckload services, as well as expedited transportation services. It also provides various value-added services, including container drayage, truckload brokerage, and supply chain consulting. In addition, the company operates fleet maintenance centers. As of December 31, 2025, it owned and operated 10,184 tractors, 30,824 linehaul trailers, and 14,313 pickup and delivery trailers. Old Dominion Freight Line, Inc. was founded in 1934 and is headquartered in Thomasville, North Carolina.
